exercises.code:
import time

pages = {
    1: {"items": ["s1", "s2"], "next": 2},
    2: {"items": ["s3"], "next": 3},
    3: {"items": ["s4"], "next": None},
}

def fetch_page(n):
    return pages[n]

all_items = []
page = 1
delays = 0
while page is not None:
    data = fetch_page(page)
    all_items.extend(data["items"])
    page = data["next"]
    if page is not None:
        delays += 1  # simula rate-limit sleep
print("items", all_items, "rate_limit_pauses", delays)
print('ok')

exercises.code:
import os, hashlib, time, json

os.environ["API_TOKEN"] = "demo-token-not-real"

def get_token():
    tok = os.environ.get("API_TOKEN")
    if not tok:
        raise RuntimeError("API_TOKEN missing")
    return tok

CACHE = {}
def cached_get(url, ttl=60):
    key = hashlib.sha256(url.encode()).hexdigest()[:12]
    now = time.time()
    if key in CACHE and now - CACHE[key]["ts"] < ttl:
        return CACHE[key]["body"], True
    body = {"url": url, "ok": True}
    CACHE[key] = {"ts": now, "body": body}
    return body, False

token = get_token()
body, hit = cached_get("https://api.example.com/signals")
prov = {
    "source_url": "https://api.example.com/signals",
    "fetched_at": "2026-07-20T12:00:00Z",
    "cache_hit": hit,
    "auth": "bearer",
    # token NEVER in provenance dump
}
print("token_len", len(token))
print("cache_hit", hit)
print("provenance", json.dumps(prov, sort_keys=True))
body2, hit2 = cached_get("https://api.example.com/signals")
print("second_hit", hit2)
print('ok')

    return tok

CACHE = {}

    key = hashlib.sha256(url.encode()).hexdigest()[:12]
    now = time.time()
    if key in CACHE and now - CACHE[key]["ts"] < ttl:
        return CACHE[key]["body"], True
    body = {"url": url, "ok": True}
    CACHE[key] = {"ts": now, "body": body}
    return body, False

token = get_token()
body, hit = cached_get("https://api.example.com/signals")
prov = {
    "source_url": "https://api.example.com/signals",
    "fetched_at": "2026-07-20T12:00:00Z",
    "cache_hit": hit,
    "auth": "bearer",
    # token NEVER in provenance dump
}
print("token_len", len(token))
print("cache_hit", hit)
print("provenance", json.dumps(prov, sort_keys=True))

exercises.code:
import math

def valid_coord(lat, lon):
    return -90 <= lat <= 90 and -180 <= lon <= 180

def haversine_km(a, b):
    R = 6371.0
    lat1, lon1 = map(math.radians, a)
    lat2, lon2 = map(math.radians, b)
    dlat, dlon = lat2 - lat1, lon2 - lon1
    h = math.sin(dlat/2)**2 + math.cos(lat1)*math.cos(lat2)*math.sin(dlon/2)**2
    return 2 * R * math.asin(math.sqrt(h))

lima = (-12.0464, -77.0428)
callao = (-12.0500, -77.1250)
print("valid", valid_coord(*lima))
print("invalid", valid_coord(91, 0))
d = haversine_km(lima, callao)
print("km_approx", round(d, 2))
print("signal_only", "relationship_signal not kinship")
print('ok')

import math

def haversine_km(a, b):
    R = 6371.0
    lat1, lon1 = map(math.radians, a)
    lat2, lon2 = map(math.radians, b)
    dlat = lat2 - lat1
    dlon = lon2 - lon1
    h = math.sin(dlat/2)**2 + math.cos(lat1)*math.cos(lat2)*math.sin(dlon/2)**2
    return 2 * R * math.asin(math.sqrt(h))

print(round(haversine_km((-12.0464, -77.0428), (-12.05, -77.04)), 3))

selfcheck.justification_from_packet: Self-check #0: «Un 400 Bad Request del proveedor debe…». Elegí el índice 3: «Tratarse como error de cliente (no retry ciego)». La teoría de la sección respalda esa lectura y descarta las distracciones.
Para la pregunta 1 («¿Dónde debe vivir el token de API?») la opción correcta es 1 — «En variable de entorno / secret store» — porque el paquete define ese contrato y no las alternativas.
En autoevaluación, pregunta 2: me quedo con la opción 2 («Inyección / inseguro; usar placeholders `?`»). Las otras no coinciden con el vocabulario del theory/iDo.
Pregunta 3 del self-check: «Enviar document_id bancario a un geocoder público…» → respuesta 0 «Viola la política de egress de CP-N1-C», alineada a outcomes y párrafos de la sección activa.

Self-check #4: «1.2 km entre dos entidades sintéticas implica…». Elegí el índice 3: «Una geoseñal de relación, no un veredicto». La teoría de la sección respalda esa lectura y descarta las distracciones.
